extra.reasoning: The property requires GLOBALLY(opted_out implies not sent), i.e., no reachable state may have opted_out=true and sent=true. The transition [t_resume_glitch] on send_attempt with guard (opted_out and ticks>=3) sets sent:=true, which directly creates such a forbidden state. This guard is reachable: three tick events drive ticks 0->1->2->3 (capped at 3), then an optout event sets opted_out=true (and sent=false, ticks unchanged at 3). A subsequent send_attempt now has both [t_send_blocked] (guard opted_out) and [t_resume_glitch] (guard opted_out and ticks>=3) enabled; under standard nondeterministic LTS semantics the system may take t_resume_glitch, setting sent=true while opted_out remains true. The resulting state (opted_out=true, sent=true) violates opted_out implies not sent. The counterexample uses 5 events, within the 7-event bound.
